title: cerastium semidecandrum subsp. fallax
intro: Cerastium semidecandrum subsp. fallax is a subspecies of the dusty miller plant, characterized by its gray-green foliage and white or pink flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It is believed to offer several health benefits, including the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The leaves and flowers of Cerastium semidecandrum subsp. fallax can be prepared as teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
